title: hieracium murorum subsp. atratum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. atratum, also known as Wall Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Wall Hawkweed can be used as a tincture, tea, or infusion, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
